'''Jayden Nikorima''' (born 5 October 1996) is a New Zealand [[rugby league]] footballer who is a player {{rlp|so}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].

Nikorima previously played for the [[Sydney Roosters]] and [[Melbourne Storm]] in the [[National Rugby League]] (NRL).

==Background==
Nikorima was born in [[Palmerston North]], New Zealand. He is of [[Māori people|Māori]] and [[Ireland|Irish]] descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://au.sports.yahoo.com/league/a/34923753/7th-tackle-luke-keary-in-line-for-world-cup-gig-with-ireland/ |title=Luke Keary in line for World Cup gig with Ireland |publisher=Yahoo |date=6 April 2017 |access-date=6 April 2017}}</ref>

His older brother [[Kodi Nikorima]] is also a professional rugby league footballer, playing for the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].<ref>{{cite news |url=https://i.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/warriors/112432737/done-deal-warriors-to-confirm-signing-of-kodi-nikorima-on-friday |title=Done deal: Warriors to confirm signing of Kodi Nikorima on Friday |publisher=stuff |date= 3 May 2019 |access-date= 3 May 2019 }}</ref>

Nikorima moved to [[Brisbane]], Queensland, Australia as a 10-year old and played his junior football for the [[Aspley Broncos|Aspley Devils]] before being signed by the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/junior-kangaroos-jayden-nikorima-and-joe-ofahengaue-are-kiwi-born-but-proud-to-wear-australias-colours/news-story/6b976dacd83a8ff3940fd64ec205fe5d|title=Junior Kangaroos Jayden Nikorima and Joe Ofahengaue are Kiwi Born but Proud to Wear Australia Colours|website=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/broncos-confident-kodi-and-jayden-nikorima-will-sign-new-deals-to-stay-at-the-club/news-story/3e225efc2b4bf1983935ee61c705de29|title=Home | Live Scores & Latest News|work=[[Fox Sports]]|date=19 April 2015|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref> Nikorima was educated at [[Wavell State High School]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/holden-cup-grand-final-2014-kodi-and-jayden-nikorima-set-to-lead-broncos-to-glory/news-story/96869049532fdbd5e564ef765eabca7b|title=Kodi and Jayden Nikorima Set to Lead Broncos to Glory|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref> In 2013, Nikorima played for the [[Queensland rugby league team|Queensland]] Under-18s and the  [[Australian Schoolboys rugby league team|Australian Schoolboys]] representative teams respectively.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.foxsportspulse.com/assoc_page.cgi?c=1-2130-0-0-0&sID=26416&news_task=DETAIL&articleID=24540576|title=Australian Schoolboys Team Announced - Australian Secondary Schools Rugby League|publisher=Fox Sports Pulse|date=2013-11-16|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
In 2014 and 2015, Nikorima played for the [[Brisbane Broncos]] [[NRL Under-20s|Holden Cup]] team. On 28 April 2014, Nikorima was named as 18th man for the [[Queensland under-20 rugby league team|Queensland Under-20s]] team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/queensland-name-under-20s-origin-team/tabid/10874/newsid/78002/default.aspx|title=Queensland name Under-20s Origin team|website=NRL.com|date=2014-04-28|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref> On 5 October 2014, Nikorima played for the Broncos in their [[2014 National Youth Competition (rugby league) season#Grand final|2014 Holden Cup Grand Final]] against the [[New Zealand Warriors]], playing off the interchange bench, scoring a try and kicking 2 goals in the 34-32 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/holden-cup-grand-final-2014-kodi-and-jayden-nikorima-set-to-lead-broncos-to-glory/story-fni3gv5x-1227079962809|title=Kodi and Jayden Nikorima Set to Lead Broncos to Glory|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/warriors-survive-broncos-scare-to-win-holden-cup/tabid/10874/newsid/82367/default.aspx|title=Warriors survive Broncos scare to win Holden Cup|website=NRL.com|date=2014-10-05|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref> On 2 May 2015, Nikorima played for the [[Junior Kangaroos]] against the [[Junior Kiwis]], playing off the interchange bench in the 22-20 win at [[Cbus Super Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.couriermail.com.au/sport/nrl/video-australias-joe-ofahengaue-dumped-on-his-head-as-junior-kangaroos-beat-junior-kiwis/news-story/cabcd6da700cf4b3c3050122beabd347|title=Kodi and Jayden Nikorima Set to Lead Broncos to Glory|website=Couriermail.com.au|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref> On 8 July 2015, Nikorima represented the [[Queensland under-20 rugby league team|Queensland Under-20s]] team, playing at five-eighth in the 32-16 loss to the [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] under-20s team at [[Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/kelly-scores-four-as-blues-win-20s/tabid/10874/newsid/87757/default.aspx|title=Kelly scores four as Blues win 20s|website=NRL.com|date=2015-07-08|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref> On 25 August 2015, it was announced that Nikorima had signed with the [[Sydney Roosters]] on a 3-year deal starting from 2016, after months of speculation as to which club Nikorima would end up at.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/teams/broncos/jayden-nikorima-signs-threeyear-deal-with-sydney-roosters/news-story/567f03be8840d269b393c384ede6ea81|title=Jayden Nikorima Signs Three Year Deal with Sydney Roosters|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/signing-of-jayden-nikorima-gives-sydney-roosters-another-win-over-brisbane-broncos-20150825-gj7mx7.html|title=Signing of Jayden Nikorima Gives Sydney Roosters Another Win Over Brisbane Broncos|website=Smh.com.au|date=25 August 2015 |access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref> On 14 September 2015, Nikorima was named on the interchange bench in the [[2015 NRL Under-20s season|2015 Holden Cup]] Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/2015-holden-cup-team-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/89792/default.aspx|title=2015 Holden Cup Team of the Year|website=NRL.com|date=14 September 2015|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref>

===2016===
On 6 January, Nikorima was selected in the [[Queensland Academy of Sport|QAS]] under-20s Origin squad.<ref>{{cite web|author=|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2016/01/06/broncos_players_domi.html|title=Broncos young guns dominate QAS U20s|website=QRL.com.au|date=6 January 2016|access-date=2016-03-05}}</ref> Nikorima made his first team debut for the [[Sydney Roosters]] in the [[World Club Series]] against match [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/nikorima-stars-as-roosters-thrash-st-helens/tabid/10874/newsid/93024/default.aspx|title=Nikorima stars as Roosters thrash St Helens|website=NRL.com|date=2016-02-20|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Cartwright|first=Phil|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/35588774|title=World Club Series: St Helens 12-38 Sydney Roosters|publisher=[[BBC Sport]]|date=2016-02-19|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref> In Round 1 of the [[2016 NRL season]], Nikorima made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut against the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]], playing at five-eighth in the Roosters 42-10 loss at the [[Sydney Football Stadium (1988)|Sydney Football Stadium]]. In Round 2 against the [[Canberra Raiders]], Nikorima scored his first NRL career try in the Roosters 21-20 loss at [[Canberra Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/injury-hit-raiders-steal-comeback-win/tabid/10874/newsid/93715/default.aspx|title=Injury-hit Raiders steal comeback win|publisher=NRL.com|date=2016-03-12|access-date=2016-04-10}}</ref> His season was hampered by hamstring injuries, which limited his appearances to just seven first grade games.<ref>{{cite web |title=Pre-season spotlight - Jayden Nikorima |url=https://www.roosters.com.au/news/2016/11/07/pre-season-spotlight--jayden-nikorima/ |website=roosters.com.au |publisher=[[Sydney Roosters]] |access-date=10 August 2022 |date=7 November 2016}}</ref>

===2017===
In the 2017 season, he spent most of his time with [[Sydney Roosters]] feeder club, [[Wyong Roos]].

In December 2017 Nikorima failed a second drug test and was sacked by the [[Sydney Roosters]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/the-66-scandals-in-four-years-that-have-rocked-the-nrl/news-story/1bbcdffd432063bec8e521c736f7ae4d/|title=The 66 scandals in four years that have rocked the NRL|website=Fox Sports|date=8 February 2019 }}</ref> The [[National Rugby League|NRL]] handed down a 12-match suspension to Nikorima that would need to be served in 2018 before he could resume playing.<ref>{{cite web |title=Sydney Roosters sack Jayden Nikorima after he cops 12-game ban for second failed drugs test |url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/99904351/sydney-roosters-sack-jayden-nikorima-after-he-cops-12game-ban-for-second-failed-drugs-test |website=stuff.co.nz |access-date=10 August 2022 |date=15 December 2017}}</ref> [[New South Wales Police Force|NSW Police]] would charge Nikorima with wilfully making a false declaration for material benefit after he had asked Wyong Roos teammate Brad Keighran to sign a statutory declaration to lie about spiking his drink. Keighran was found guilty for his part in the plot, and was sentenced to six months imprisonment, but served his term by way of an intensive community corrections order.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Proszenko |first1=Adrian |title=Former Roosters playmaker charged over drink spike lie |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/former-roosters-playmaker-charged-over-drink-spike-lie-20181201-p50jlr.html |website=smh.com.au |publisher=Nine Media |access-date=10 August 2022 |date=1 December 2018}}</ref> Nikorima would also avoid imprisonment, instead serving a 12-month community corrections order after being convicted and fined $5,000. It was reported that for failing the drugs test he had cost himself close to $1m when he was sacked by the Roosters.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Pengilly |first1=Adam |title=Former NRL player avoids jail over Mad Monday drink spike lie |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/former-nrl-player-avoids-jail-over-mad-monday-drink-spike-lie-20190320-p515nn.html |website=smh.com.au |publisher=Nine Media |access-date=10 August 2022 |date=20 March 2019}}</ref>

===2019===
He was signed with [[Brisbane Broncos]] feeder club, [[Redcliffe Dolphins]] in late 2018,<ref>{{cite web |title=Redcliffe Dolphins on Twitter |url=https://twitter.com/DolphinsRLFC/status/1067638797389651968 |website=twitter.com |access-date=10 August 2022 |date=28 November 2018}}</ref> joining the club in 2019 to play in the [[Queensland Cup]].

===2021===

In November 2021 he signed with [[Melbourne Storm]] for 1 year.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2021/11/04/storm-sign-olympic-games-medal-winner/ | title=Storm sign Olympic Games medal winner | date=3 November 2021 }}</ref>

===2022===

On 1 February 2022, Nikorima was named to make his [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] debut against the [[Indigenous All-Stars (rugby league)|Indigenous All Stars]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2022/02/01/2022-harvey-norman-all-stars-teams-announced/|title=2022 Harvey Norman All Stars teams announced|date=February 2022}}</ref>

In round 16, Nikorima made his [[Melbourne Storm]] debut against the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] after playing his last NRL match over 2,264 days ago. He had his Storm debut jersey (cap 223) presented to him by teammate [[Cameron Munster]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Nikorima becomes player #223 |url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2022/06/30/nikorima-becomes-player-223/ |website=melbournestorm.com.au |access-date=25 October 2022 |date=30 June 2022}}</ref>

===2023===
In round 27 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Nikorima was called into the Melbourne side for their game against Brisbane which Melbourne would go on to win 32-22.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.espn.co.uk/nrl/story/_/id/38296972/nrl-round-27-storm-hammer-broncos-nrl-minor-premiership-hopes/|title=Storm hammer Broncos' NRL minor premiership hopes|website=www.espn.co.uk}}</ref>

On 30 September 2023, it was reported that Nikorima had signed for French side [[Catalans Dragons]] on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.msn.com/en-gb/sport/rugbyleague/nrl-playmaker-jayden-nikorima-makes-super-league-move-following-release-from-melbourne-storm/ar-AA1htU2i?ocid=msedgntp&cvid=d2e1db49418a4b748389e072bf410f99&ei=27|title=NRL playmaker Jayden Nikorima makes Super League move following release from Melbourne Storm|website=Love Rugby League|date=30 Sep 2023}}</ref>

===2024===
On 12 July 2024 it was announced that [[Catalans Dragons]] had terminated his contract, along with two other players namely [[Sio Siua Taukeiaho]] and [[Damel Diakhate]], for being ''"...involved in an incident deemed to be ‘highly unacceptable’ by the French club and contrary to their values..."''.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=12 Jul 2024|title=Catalans Dragons terminate contracts of three players including high-profile duo after 'incident'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/catalans-dragons-terminate-contracts-of-three-players}}</ref> On 18 July, he signed a deal with the [[Salford Red Devils]] to the end of the 2025 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/every-super-league-clubs-quota-spots-situation-in-2024 |title=Every Super League club's overseas quota spots situation after Salford Red Devils confirm Jayden Nikorima arrival |website=Love Rugby League |date=18 July 2024 |access-date=27 July 2024}}</ref>

===2025===
Nikorima played 15 matches for Salford in the 2025 Super League season as the club endured a horrid year on and off the field finishing bottom of the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.prolificnorth.co.uk/news/struggling-salford-red-devils-hit-with-two-point-deduction-while-club-addresses-weekend-fan-protests/|title=Salford Red Devils dealt double blow as nightmare 2025 takes another turn|website=www.prolificnorth.co.uk}}</ref>

On 23 October 2025 it was announced that Nikorima has signed a three-year contract with newly promoted [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=23 October 2025 |title=Bradford sign half-back Nikorima|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn51xxz4qv4o#:~:text=Bradford%20Bulls%20have%20signed%20Salford,the%202024%20Super%20League%20season.|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
